highlight.question: What restaurant grants fit kitchen equipment in the Netherlands?
highlight.answer:
For food-service businesses, these schemes mention your sector.

For **restaurant and hospitality** businesses, these **8** schemes mention your sector in our catalogue.

Scheme cards are on the right — plus quick links if you want to browse the full restaurant portal or finance finder.

**Good to know**
- Hospitality and restaurant grants in one catalogue — filter by region, topic, and deadline before you shortlist schemes in chat.
- Each row links to official programme detail; select two schemes here or in chat to compare side by side.
- Pair with finance finder when non-repayable support does not cover full equipment capex.

**Worked example:** **Restaurant schemes catalogue** — Browse hospitality grants — filter by region before comparing in chat.

**Site example:** NL equipment capex often stacks **MIA & Vamil** — *Dutch tax schemes for companies investing in environment-friendly business assets or technology (Environmental invest…* — verify asset lists and Belastingdienst rules on the official page.

_Greenways focuses on Netherlands and UK operators; set your region filter for tighter matches. Our catalogue is one master **Schemes** list — marketplace product pages show mat…

highlight.question: How do energy prices affect upgrade payback for my restaurant?
highlight.answer:
Wholesale moves are a signal; your contract still sets the bill you pay.

:::profile-context
Schemes, examples, and links below are filtered for **Netherlands** · **Restaurant**.
:::



**Energy prices (Netherlands)** — wholesale €/MWh helps you time upgrades and tariff reviews. Your bill also depends on supplier, pass-through clauses, and time-of-use, so retail contracts can move differently from the ticker.

**Netherlands** — €122.76/MWh wholesale (▼1.2% vs prior)

Site modelling uses **€0.30/kWh** electricity · gas ~€1.16/m³ for payback examples.

When unit costs rise, **ETL-listed equipment** lowers the kWh you still buy — open the modules on the right for the ticker, site utility view, and tariff compare. From there, the **Product Calculator** lets you compare the energy use of **efficient equipment** vs what you run today.

**Good to know**
- Live wholesale €/MWh lanes — same feed as the Greenways buildings dashboard — for timing upgrades and tariff reviews.
- Retail bills can lag wholesale by weeks; use this as an early signal, not your contract rate.
- Pair with savings trajectory when usage and unit costs both trend up.

**Worked example:** **Electricity utility view** —…

highlight.question: Where should I start with insulation and equipment upgrades?
highlight.answer:
Envelope upgrades usually beat adding more kitchen capex on a leaky building.

:::profile-context
Schemes, examples, and links below are filtered for **Netherlands** · **Restaurant**.
:::



**Insulation & building fabric** — fabric, HVAC, and efficient equipment work together when you are chasing sustainability targets and lower running costs.

Green retrofits raise property value, unlock grants, cut operating costs, and improve climate resilience — not only environmental benefit.

Fabric and insulation lower baseload before oversized equipment capex. Climate-proofing (flood, heat, storm) protects asset value alongside kWh savings.

**Hospitality note:** Hospitality buildings often use 5-7× more energy per m² than other commercial stock — smart layout and ventilation design can cut costs while improving service flow.

The renovation guides on the right cover retrofit pathways, payback examples, and kitchen design savings. When you want payback modelling, **Vincent** can help; for scheme eligibility, **Andrieus** has the catalogue.

**Good to know**
- Explains why envelope and fabric upgrades should precede heavy HVAC or kitchen capex — less heat loss means smaller equipment specs…

highlight.question: What interesting deals are in the feed right now?
highlight.answer:
Let me pull what's live in the deals feed for that.

I checked the **deals feed** just now (last built **2026-07-15**).

There are **11** curated rows — **4** flagged as new and **1** product spotlight.

On the right are live examples for **Netherlands** — open a card for tariffs, water savings, or product spotlights on the live portal.

_Prices and contract terms change — confirm on the linked page before switching._

**Good to know**
- Three marquee lanes — energy, water, and sustainability — plus manual search when you know the supplier or product.
- Category pills filter lanes; spotlight cards jump to tariff compare or product detail.
- Watch scrolling lanes for timely offers, then open the full Deals page for sidebar portals without losing context.

**Worked example:** **Energy deals ticker** — Scrolling energy supply lanes — pair with tariff compare when renewing.

**Site example:** The **deals ticker hub** runs three lanes — **energy**, **water**, and **sustainability** — plus search; feed rows are curated entry points, so always confirm price and contract terms on the linked portal.

_Product deal spotlights (weekly offers with productId) live in the sustainability lane —…

highlight.question: What's in today's sustainability news briefing?
highlight.answer:
Let me explain what's happening in sustainability and how it could help your site.

Here is **today's sustainability briefing** (built **2026-07-05**) (curated from **2026-06** edition) with a **Netherlands** restaurant lens.

I pulled **5 headlines** from our curated news catalogue — not live-scraped from the web in this chat. Open Cheryce’s **newsletter panels** on the right for each headline; pair with the **energy ticker** when wholesale moves change your upgrade timing.

Want me to explain a term (CBAM, CSRD) or tie a headline to the **sustainability map**?

**Good to know**
- Sustainability News Roundup — June 2026.
- Top Stories.
- 🌍 CBAM: two months to the annual declaration.

**Site example:** Today's briefing pulls **5** headlines from **2026-06** — including **⚡ Energy Omnibus published (24 June 2026)** — pair with the energy ticker when wholesale moves change upgrade timing.

_Each story includes a short **how this helps you** line — open an edition card when you want the full newsletter._

highlight.question: How do I use the sensor dashboard and buildings overview?
highlight.answer:
Greenways has a buildings dashboard — KPI tiles, trends, and utility drill-down.

:::profile-context
Schemes, examples, and links below are filtered for **Netherlands** · **Restaurant**.
:::



**Greenways energy dashboard**

Greenways buildings dashboard — ./Greenways%20Interface%20.html

_Status: Portfolio KPIs and charts — embed/display still being finalised; not fully live on Render yet._

**What it shows for restaurant:**
- KPI tiles: Electricity, Gas, Water, Cost Today — link to utility-detail
- 7-day trend + usage breakdown + AI insights row
- Baseline vs actual targets and cost-leak detector on analytics
- Equipment feed from site assets — efficiency score vs fleet average
- System status panel (Live / Hold / Feed issue)

**Drill-down:**
- **Utility detail** — ./utility-detail.html?type=electricity|gas|water
- **Sensor dashboard** — ./sensor-dashboard.html
- **Equipment deep dive** — ./restaurant-equipment-deep-dive.html (product-level systems math)

**Tariff model behind the numbers:** Electricity €0.30/kWh · Gas ~€1.16/m³ (10.55 kWh(th)/m³ × €0.11/kWh(th)) · Water €2.50/m³ (€0.0025/L)
